rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSITION: ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Petitioners First Set Of Demands For The Production And Inspection Of Documents, First Set Of Special Interrogatories, And First Set Of Form Interrogatories; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Amy Shearer; Declaration Of Tracy Sacca [Proposed Order]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 18, 2019, Line 1 - PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA's Motion To Compel Answers To Petitioners First Set Of Demands For The Production And Inspection Of Documents, First Set Of Special Interrogatories, And Form Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to compel is GRANTED. Claimant did not respond to the Form Interrogatories (First Set), Special Interrogatories (First Set), Demands for Production (First Set), and did not timely file an opposition to the motion to compel. The Sacca Declaration sets out reasonable attempts to effect service on Claimant. All objections are waived. CCP 2030.290, 2031.300. Claimant is ordered to respond to all pending discovery requests, without objections, no later than August 2, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(JPT/302)

rulings.Case Title: WISHING WELL FARMS VS. FARMGIRL FLOWERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 18, 2019, Line 2, PETITIONER WISHING WELL FARMS' Motion To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award. Petitioner Wishing Well Farms' motion to confirm contractual arbitration award is granted. (Code Civ. Proc. § 1286.) Respondent Farmgirl Flowers does not contend that there are grounds for vacating the award. (Code Civ. Proc. § 1286.2.) Petitioner's request to introduce oral evidence at the hearing is denied. (Cal. R. Ct. 3.1306(a).) The Court will enter judgment on the award in the amount of $146,572.20. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the petitioner is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and a separate proposed judgment and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JAY FINK VS. CLICKSYNDICATE.COM, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Special Motion And Motion To Strike First Amended Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 18, 2019, Line 3, CROSS DEFENDANTS JAY FINK, ANGELA NEILSON's Special Motion To Strike First Amended Cross Complaint. Cross-defendants Jay Fink and Angela Neilson's special motion to strike the first amended cross-complaint by Cross-Complainants Altare Publishing Inc., Clicksyndicate.com LLC, and Fairlight Scientific LLC pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure §425.16 is denied. The motion is untimely. Absent court permission, a special motion to strike must be filed within 60 days of service of the original pleading. (Code Civ. Proc. § 425.16(f).) "'An amended complaint reopens the time to file an anti-SLAPP motion without court permission only if the amended complaint pleads new causes of action that could not have been the target of a prior anti-SLAPP motion, or adds new allegations that make previously pleaded causes of action subject to an anti-SLAPP motion.'" (Newport Harbor Ventures, LLC v. Morris Cerullo World Evangelism (2018) 4 Cal.5th 637, 641; see also id. at 646 ["An amended complaint might not add new causes of action subject to an anti-SLAPP motion, in which case no anti-SLAPP motion at all would be permitted"].) The court's full tentative ruling was emailed to the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the cross-complainants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: RANDY SCHRECK VS. NEA DELIVERY LLC DBA AIRSPACE TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Psych Ime Of Plaintiff Randy Schreck
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 18, 2019, Line 4 - DEFENDANT NEA DELIVERY LLC's Motion To Compel Psych Ime Of Plaintiff Randy Schreck. Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion for a mental examination is DENIED. Defendants do not show good cause under CCP 2032.320(a). While Plaintiff alleges anxiety and other neurological damages following the accident, Defendants do not articulate why they need a mental examination in addition to routine discovery of medical records. Moreover, the moving papers and meet-and-confer correspondence were unclear what diagnostic tests or procedures were to be done, or why those procedures would lead to evidence that would be relevant to any claims or defenses. CCP 2032.320(d). Defendants submission of Dr. Stassberg's declaration with the reply is improper, especially considering that the information in that declaration could and should have been provided in the meet and confer process. Jay v. Mahaffey (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 1522, 1537-38. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(JPT/302)

rulings.Case Title: KATHLEEN RODANTE VS. WHISTLE LABS INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 18, 2019, Line 8, DEFENDANT WHISTLE LABS INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Whistle Lab's demurrer to the sixth cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress is sustained. Worker's compensation exclusivity does not bar a claim for intentional infliction of emotional distress based upon discriminatory or retaliatory conduct because such conduct is outside the normal risk of the compensation bargain. (See Light v. Department of Parks & Recreation (2017) 14 Cal.App.5th 75, 101 ["unlawful discrimination and retaliation in violation of FEHA falls outside the compensation bargain and therefore claims of intentional infliction of emotional distress based on such discrimination and retaliation are not subject to workers' compensation exclusivity"]; Nazir v. United Airlines, Inc. (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 243, 288; Fretland v. County of Humboldt (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 1478, 1492.) The court's full tentative ruling was emailed to the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: CYNTHIA HILL VS. MARRIOTT INTERNATIONAL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 18, 2019, Line 11, DEFENDANTS MARRIOTT INTERNATIONAL, INC., STARWOOD HOTELS & RESORTS WORLDWIDE, LLC, W HOTEL MANAGEMENT, INC., ROGER HULDI, JULIO FERNANDEZ's Motion To Compel Arbitration. Defendants Marriott International, Inc., Starwood Hotels & Resorts Worldwide, LLC, W Hotel Management, Inc., Roger Huldi, and Julio Fernandez's motion to compel arbitration is granted. Plaintiff Cynthia Hill admits that she signed the arbitration agreement. (Hill Supp. Decl. 6.) By submitting the Declaration of Human Resources Director Wendy Hunter and the Agreement and Plan of Merger, of which the Court takes judicial notice, Defendants have established that Marriott, as the acquirer of Starwood, may enforce the arbitration agreement as a nonsignatory successor-in-interest. (See RJN Ex. 1 [merger agreement]; Hunter Decl. 6 ["In September 2016, Marriott acquired Starwood Hotels & Resorts Worldwide, Inc., which included the W San Francisco."]; Jenks v. DLA Piper Rudnick Gray Cary US LLP (2015) 243 Cal.App.4th 1, 9 [allowing nonsignatory successor-in-interest to enforce arbitration agreement]; Marenco v. DirecTV LLC (2015) 233 Cal.App.4th 1409, 1417 [nonsignatory defendant may enforce arbitration agreement between signatory plaintiff and a corporation that was acquired by the nonsignatory defendant].) The court's full tentative ruling was emailed to the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ARCON CONSTRUCTION CORPORATION VS. 653 28TH STREET LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Notice Of Hearing To Stay State Court Proceedings In Their Entirety Pending The Outcome Of Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 18, 2019, Line 9, DEFENDANT 653 28TH STREET LLC's Motion To Stay State Court Proceedings In Their Entirety Pending The Outcome Of Arbitration. Defendant 653 28th Street, LLC's motion to stay is denied. Contrary to Defendant's contention, Code of Civil Procedure Section 1281.4 does not mandate a stay of the litigation. Section 1281.4 applies only "[i]f a court of competent jurisdiction . . . has ordered arbitration of a controversy which is an issue involved in an action or proceeding pending before a court of this State," or if "an application has been made to a court of competent jurisdiction . . . for an order to arbitrate" such a controversy. Thus, the statutory language "requires a court to grant a party's motion to stay when the court has ordered arbitration of an issue in the action." (Thomas v.Westlake (2012) 204 Cal.App.4th 605, 620 (emphasis added).) Here, no such court order was sought or entered. Further, the contractual arbitration is currently scheduled for December 2019, and the first amended complaint in this action was only recently filed and served. Accordingly, on the current record, the Court does not believe there is a significant risk of inconsistent rulings; accordingly, Plaintiff Arcon Construction Corporation's request pursuant to Section 1281.2 to stay the arbitration pending the outcome of this court proceeding is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: QCP CAPITAL PTE, LTD. VS. AUTOLOTTO, INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 18, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANT AUTOLOTTO, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Autolotto, Inc.'s demurrer to complaint is overruled. The fraud cause of action is alleged with sufficient particularity. Plaintiff alleges that it was duped into entering into the promissory notes because Defendant misrepresented that Beth Bresnahan (former director of Massachusetts lottery) and Joey Krup (manager of Pantera Fund) were advisors to Defendant. The complaint alleges that the misrepresentations were on defendant's website and its online "White Paper." The complaint even attaches actual copies of the alleged misrepresentations. The Court fails to see what further facts need to be alleged to support the claim. (See West v. JPMorgan Chase Bank, NA (2013) 214 Cal.App.4th 780, 793 [plaintiff met specificity requirement where she alleged where and when defendant made misrepresentations, including in documents attached to the complaint].) Lastly, whether the misrepresentations are actually material raises a factual issue. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
